key.name: control-port
key.description: TCP port on 127.0.0.1 where tor's ControlPort listens.

key.name: cookie-path
key.description: Path to tor's control auth cookie (32 raw bytes). ~ expands to $HOME at runtime.

key.name: control-password
key.description: Cleartext password used if cookie auth is unavailable. Matches HashedControlPassword in torrc.

key.name: manage-system-proxy
key.description: OFF by default. When enabled, start an embedded PAC server and set org.gnome.system.proxy mode=auto with autoconfig-url pointing at it — this works for libproxy-aware apps (Discord, Evolution) and Firefox with 'Use system proxy settings'. Chrome on recent Linux often ignores it entirely, which is why the default is off and users are pointed at the SOCKS endpoint shown in the tile.

key.name: pac-port
key.description: Port on 127.0.0.1 where the embedded proxy-autoconfig server listens.

key.name: saved-autoconfig-url
key.summary: Internal: saved autoconfig-url before enabling Tor

key.name: default-exit-country
key.description: Two-letter country code for ExitNodes. Empty means any country.

key.name: bridge-lines
key.description: One bridge line per entry, e.g. "obfs4 1.2.3.4:443 FINGERPRINT cert=... iat-mode=0".

key.name: saved-proxy-mode
key.summary: Internal: saved proxy mode before enabling Tor

key.name: saved-socks-host
key.summary: Internal: saved SOCKS host before enabling Tor

key.name: saved-socks-port
key.summary: Internal: saved SOCKS port before enabling Tor

key.name: saved-use-same-proxy
key.summary: Internal: saved use-same-proxy before enabling Tor

key.name: saved-use-same-proxy-set
key.summary: Internal: whether saved-use-same-proxy contains a captured value

key.name: use-tun2socks
key.summary: Route all traffic through Tor via tun2socks (transparent proxy)
key.description: When enabled, ALL system TCP traffic is pushed through a TUN device that tun2socks forwards to tor's SOCKS5 port. DNS is redirected to tor's DNSPort. Requires one-time privileged setup (scripts/install-tun2socks.sh) that creates a dedicated _tor-ext system user and installs system units + a polkit rule so the tile stays passwordless at runtime. Enabling this also switches tor itself from the user-mode unit to the system-mode unit so its own outbound traffic can be exempted by uid.

key.name: tun-address
key.summary: IPv4 address/prefix assigned to the TUN interface

key.name: tor-dns-port
key.summary: Tor DNSPort (used by tun2socks DNS redirection)

key.name: tor-system-cookie-path
key.summary: Cookie auth path for the system-mode tor unit
key.description: Used when use-tun2socks is on. The _tor-ext group is group-readable; the installer adds the login user to that group.
